tasks.name: Register for the cheapest Chicago alumni networking event next month under $40
tasks.description: Find an alumni networking event in Chicago happening next calendar month, with a ticket price under $40, and complete a registration form selecting the cheapest qualifying ticket while specifying Technology as the industry and 2015 as the graduation year.
steps: Navigate to the alumni website homepage.
In the main navigation, click on the "Events" or "Upcoming Events" section.
Use the location filter or search field to select or type "Chicago" as the event city and apply the filter.
Open the date filter and set the start date to the 1st day of next calendar month and the end date to the last day of next calendar month, then apply the date range.
Locate the event type or category filter and select the checkbox or option for "Networking" events, then apply the filter.
Open the sort dropdown for events and choose "Price – Low to High" so that the cheapest events appear first.
From the filtered and sorted results, click on the first event whose listed ticket price is under $40 to open its detail page.
On the event detail page, click the "Register" or "Sign Up" button and, if multiple ticket types are shown, select the ticket type with the lowest price that is still under $40.

In the registration form, fill in your first and last name, enter "alum.chicago@example.com" as the email address, select "2015" as the graduation year, and choose "Technology" as the industry or professional field.
Select any required consent or terms checkbox and click the "Submit Registration" or "Complete Registration" button to finalize the event registration.

tasks.name: Make a $75 one-time pledge to the Student Scholarships fund with an honor tribute
tasks.description: Submit an online pledge form to donate a one-time amount of $75 to the 'Student Scholarships' fund, specifying it is made 'In honor of' Jordan Lee and choosing an on-campus billing option rather than online card payment.
steps: Navigate to the alumni website homepage.
In the main navigation, click on the "Give" or "Make a Gift" section.
On the giving page, select the option for a "One-time gift" or similar single donation type.
In the gift amount field, type "75" to set the pledge amount to $75.
Open the fund or designation dropdown and select the option labeled "Student Scholarships" as the gift destination.
Locate the tribute or dedication section, enable the tribute checkbox, choose the "In honor of" option, and type "Jordan Lee" in the honoree name field.
In the contact information section, fill in your name, enter "donor.example@example.com" as the email, and type "12345" as the ZIP/postal code.
For the payment or billing method, select a non-card option such as "Bill me later", "Pay by mail", or "On-campus billing" (choose the option that indicates payment will not be completed online).
Select any required consent or communication preference checkboxes (for example, agreeing to terms or allowing email receipts).
Click the "Submit Pledge", "Confirm Gift", or similarly labeled button to send the $75 pledge to the Student Scholarships fund with the specified tribute.

tasks.name: Subscribe to the alumni email newsletter with monthly career and events updates
tasks.description: Sign up for the alumni email newsletter using a sample email address, selecting only the 'Career Services' and 'Alumni Events' topics and choosing a monthly digest frequency.
steps: Navigate to the alumni website homepage.
In the main navigation, hover over or click "Stay Connected" (or a similar section) and choose "Email Newsletters" or "Subscribe" from the submenu.
On the newsletter page, click the "Sign Up" or "Manage Subscription" button to open the subscription form.
In the form, type "sample.alum@example.com" into the email address field and enter a first and last name in the corresponding fields.
Use the graduation year dropdown to select "2012" as the class year and, if available, choose a school or faculty from the related dropdown.
In the topics or interests section, check the boxes for "Career Services" and "Alumni Events" and uncheck any other topic options if they are selected by default.
In the frequency or delivery options, select the radio button for "Monthly digest" (or the closest monthly option) instead of daily or weekly.
Scroll to the consent section, check the box indicating agreement to receive email communications from the alumni office.
Click the "Subscribe", "Save Preferences", or similarly labeled button to submit the newsletter subscription with the chosen topics and monthly frequency.

tasks.name: Join the California alumni chapter with a membership fee under $30 and the most events
tasks.description: Find alumni chapters in California with a membership fee of $30 or less, identify the one that shows the highest number of upcoming events, and complete its join form.
steps: Navigate to the alumni website homepage.
In the main navigation, click on "Chapters & Clubs", "Local Chapters", or a similarly named section for regional groups.
Use the location or region filter to select "California" as the state and apply the filter to show California chapters only.
If available, use a membership fee filter or slider to set the maximum fee to "$30" and apply the filter so only chapters with fees of $30 or less are listed.
Sort the visible chapters by "Upcoming Events" or "Events (Most to Least)" so that the chapters with the highest number of upcoming events appear at the top.

From the filtered and sorted list, identify the chapter in California with a listed membership fee of $30 or less and the highest count of upcoming events; if more than one has the same highest count, choose the first one in the list and click its chapter name to open the detail page.
On the chapter detail page, click the "Join Chapter" or "Become a Member" button to open the membership form.
In the membership form, enter your name, email address "chapter.joiner@example.com", and select "Alumni" as your role and "2010" as your graduation year.
If a membership fee option list is provided, select the membership option that matches the fee listed on the chapter page (which must be $30 or less).
Check any required agreement or terms checkboxes and click the "Submit", "Join", or "Complete Membership" button to finalize joining that California chapter.

tasks.name: Save two alumni travel benefits offering at least 10% discounts to your saved list
tasks.description: Browse alumni benefits, filter for travel-related offers with a discount of 10% or more, and add the first two qualifying offers to the 'Saved Benefits' or similar personal list.
steps: Navigate to the alumni website homepage.
In the main navigation, click on "Alumni Benefits" or "Benefits & Discounts" to open the benefits listing.
Use the category filter and select "Travel" (or "Travel & Transportation") to show only travel-related benefits, then apply the filter.
If a discount percentage filter is available, set the minimum discount to "10%" and apply the filter so only offers with a 10% or greater discount are displayed.
Open the sorting dropdown on the benefits list and choose "Discount – High to Low" so that the most generous travel discounts are at the top.
From the filtered results, click on the first benefit card or title that indicates a discount of at least 10% (for example, "15% off hotel stays") to open its detail page.
On the benefit detail page, click the "Save to My Benefits", "Add to Saved", or similarly labeled button to add this offer to your saved list.
Use the back button or the breadcrumb link to return to the filtered travel benefits list.
From the remaining list, click on the next benefit card that shows a discount of at least 10% (the next item in the sorted order) to open its detail page.
On the second benefit detail page, click the "Save to My Benefits", "Add to Saved", or equivalent button to add this second travel offer to your saved benefits list.

tasks.name: Add three San Francisco software engineering alumni (2010–2020) to a contact list
tasks.description: Use the public alumni directory to find alumni in San Francisco working in software engineering who graduated between 2010 and 2020, then add the first three matching alumni profiles to a 'Contact List' or similar saved list.
steps: Navigate to the alumni website homepage.
In the main navigation, click on "Alumni Directory" or "Find Alumni" to open the directory search page.
On the directory page, click the "Advanced Search" or "More Filters" option to expand additional search criteria.
In the location section, type "San Francisco" into the city field and, if required, select the appropriate state (such as "California") from a dropdown.
In the industry or job function dropdown, select an option that corresponds to software work such as "Software Engineering", "Software Development", or "Technology – Software".
In the graduation year range fields, enter "2010" as the minimum year and "2020" as the maximum year, then click the "Search" or "Apply Filters" button.
On the search results page, open the sort dropdown and select "Last Name – A to Z" or another consistent ordering so that the list order is stable.

Click on the first alumnus/alumna profile in the results list to open their detail page, then click the "Add to Contact List", "Save Contact", or similarly named button to add them to your contact list.

Use the browser back button or a "Back to Results" link to return to the filtered search results, then repeat the process by opening the second result, clicking its "Add to Contact List" or equivalent button, and returning to the results.

From the results list, open the third alumnus/alumna profile that matches the filters and click the "Add to Contact List" or similar button so that a total of three San Francisco software engineering alumni from the 2010–2020 range have been added.

tasks.name: Enroll as a mentor for current students with 2 hours per month availability
tasks.description: Compare mentoring options and register as a mentor in the program specifically serving current students, selecting Technology and Entrepreneurship as areas of expertise and setting your availability to 2 hours per month via video chat.
steps: Navigate to the alumni website homepage.
In the main navigation, click on "Volunteer & Mentor", "Get Involved", or a similar section and select the page that describes mentoring programs.

On the mentoring programs overview, identify and click on a link such as "Compare Mentoring Programs" or scroll to where different program types (e.g., current students vs recent graduates) are listed side by side.
Review the list of programs and click on the program whose audience or description explicitly states that it is for "Current students" rather than "Recent graduates" or other groups.
On the current-students mentoring program page, click the "Sign Up to Mentor", "Become a Mentor", or similarly labeled button to open the mentor registration form.
In the form, fill in your name and email address (for example, "mentor.example@example.com") and select your primary affiliation as "Alumnus/Alumna" along with a graduation year such as "2008".
In the areas of expertise or interests section, select at least two checkboxes including "Technology" and "Entrepreneurship" as your mentoring topics.
In the availability or time commitment dropdown, choose "2 hours per month" (or the closest 2-hours-per-month option) as your preferred mentoring time.
In the preferred meeting format section, select "Video chat" or an equivalent virtual meeting option as your primary format.
Check any required terms or privacy consent boxes and click the "Submit", "Enroll as Mentor", or equivalent button to complete enrollment in the current-students mentoring program.

tasks.name: Save the highest-value undergraduate scholarship for children of alumni with a deadline after March 1, 2025
tasks.description: Locate scholarships for children of alumni enrolled as undergraduates with application deadlines after March 1, 2025, sort them by award amount, select the one with the highest award, and add it to a saved scholarships list.
steps: Navigate to the alumni website homepage.
In the main navigation, click on "Scholarships & Support", "Alumni Family Scholarships", or a similar section that lists scholarships related to alumni and their families.
On the scholarships page, use the eligibility or recipient filter to select "Children of alumni" as the eligible applicant group and apply the filter.
Use the level-of-study filter to choose "Undergraduate" so that only undergraduate scholarships are displayed.
Locate the deadline or application date filter and set it so that only scholarships with an application deadline after "March 1, 2025" are shown, then apply the date filter.
Open the sort dropdown for the scholarships list and select "Award Amount – High to Low" to place the scholarships with the largest awards at the top.
Click on the first scholarship in the sorted, filtered list (the one with the highest award amount) to open its detail page.
On the scholarship detail page, click the "Save Scholarship", "Add to Favorites", or similarly labeled button to add this scholarship to your personal saved scholarships list.

If there is a notes or label field associated with saved scholarships, type "Undergrad child of alum – post 3/1/2025" into the notes field and click "Save" or "Update" to store the note with the saved scholarship.
